He said, gee, I certainly wasn't planning on spending that much money on myself. Now he actually enunciated. The real reason. The real reason. The salesman went to price value. And he went back. They didn't handle the real objection. He went back into, it's made from titanium and you know, it costs a fortune for the molds and the NASA uses the same. So he went back into all that stuff. The guy who's in his 80s of course is really saying, I got grandkids. I feel morally obligated. They get at that age, if you give them a sweater for Christmas, they take it down to get a refund. They put the money back in the box for the grandkids for when they die. I mean, that's just right. So often a price objection, even when it's said as a price objection, it's not really an objection to price. It has a different meaning. And conversely, often they'll actually state it right and a poorly skilled, non-listening salesperson will turn it into a price objection. Yeah, absolutely. And the other thing is, something that you do very well is pre-framing before the prospect even gets in front of you, they know it's not going to be cheap. Yeah, they don't have to know the exact price. But they don't necessarily. But they know that. There is a way to sell where they know the exact price. But they at least know by who they are, where they were sourced from, what they have seen in advance. And in some cases, it's stated without a number. They know that this is not going to be, it's called price desensitization. And it happens all the way along a process. You mentioned Charlie, when you walk in the dental office, you know this ain't Aspen. And really what you say to yourself is, oh, this is going to cost a little more than I thought it was. So now when the price is a little more than you had prepared yourself for, up until then, you say, oh yeah, I told myself that 22 minutes ago, you're sort of prepared for it. And then the other thing is, if you're getting that a lot and it really is price, well then you're bringing in the wrong prospects. And your marketing and your prospecting is not bringing in the right folks. Market to message match, right? You're just bringing in the wrong folks. You know, that's why I really don't personally, I don't like the standard canned answers to the price objection. Because like you said, there's typically something more to it than it's actually too much money. Nick, let's go to the next one. And again, this kind of goes to the same answer to the first one. Now by the way, in a typical sales trading course, they would give you the, you know, here are the five things to say. But this is going a little bit deeper than that. No need. No need. You know, I think of this one, you're selling against status quo, right? So some of it could be the objections. You haven't covered the objection or your presentation didn't build enough need or want in the person. We talked in the first or second segment about pain and pleasure. So you didn't move them to something or you didn't move them away from a level of pain. And so they haven't had the disconnect of not having the product. And so I really think it's really built into the presentation themselves. And I like to use pain and I think you're a big fan of it also, you know, whether it's agony or anything of that nature of moving them away from... You mean you like pain and selling? Pain and selling. Okay, I just want to clarify that. Thank you. Thank you. But moving them... He's like very serious. He's like... I have to be. He's like, I'm going to drink this cup all afternoon and it's filled with bourbon. So I'm trying to work it through. But you know, moving them either away from pain or towards pleasure. And I think at the end of the day, that is where the no need comes from. When you happen to be in a... Our poor people live like rich people and the rest of them are. Right? I mean, there's an enormous number of people you are going to sell to who really don't need anything. They could go a long time. Right? Our closets are all full. Right? We don't really need to buy anything. Most of us are probably going to buy some clothes this year. But the truth of the matter is... Right, absolutely. To make room for them, we've got to take something and give it to the goodwill just to be able to put it in the closet. Right? So, but even... So the higher up the affluence level you go in selling, the less anybody needs anything. Right? So, you have to not have this in play at all. Right? It has to be about want. It has to be about... Even though I've done some... I've tried to solve this problem 18 times before and I have 18 things that I own that have failed to solve it, this finally is the thing that will make that solution go away. But even sort of at the bottom of the economic level.
